title: Runtime planner trusted caller-controlled package digest for shard routing
trust_boundary: untrusted package metadata -> runtime planner route selection
attacker_capability_model: an external caller or compromised upstream integrator could previously submit package metadata whose tx_digest_hex diverged from the actual TxPackage or ClaimTxPackage payload
main_vulnerability: The old runtime planner path derived route_key, intake_ids, and plan_digest from caller-controlled tx_digest_hex metadata without recomputing or enforcing a canonical payload-bound digest at the planner boundary. The live code no longer does that.
implementation_nuance: Wallet-side and theorem-side verifiers remain valid compensating controls, but the fixed runtime planner boundary now recomputes and enforces the same digest locally before route selection.

evidence.summary: IngressBoundary::normalize now recomputes tx and claim digests and rejects mismatches before planning.
IntakeId fields and WorkItem construction stay private to the crate, leaving one canonical planner-ready path.
Route lookup now consumes item.route_key() from verified runtime metadata.
The public ingress trait starts from WorkPayload rather than caller-filled planner DTOs.
Release-tested source guards fail if WorkItem regains public constructors, public digest fields, or raw planner digest decoding.
The crate README now documents ingress as the only planner-ready WorkItem path and rejects caller-supplied digest strings as planner authority.

pros: the fixed runtime boundary is backed by production code, tests, and refreshed docs
route selection, ordered intake ids, and plan_digest now all depend on the same verified payload-bound digest path
the public planner lane has a release-tested source guard against bypass regression
cons: the repository still does not include a concrete AggregatorService intake implementation, so integration exposure remains bounded by downstream wiring
the claim-verify marker type stays intentionally narrow and is not itself the planner-admission owner

verification.reason: live code and release validation show runtime ingress rebinding, verified route lookup, and no safe public planner-ready bypass

defenses: recompute canonical digests inside IngressBoundary::normalize and reject mismatches
route from verified runtime metadata instead of raw IntakeId digest strings
keep forged-digest regression coverage for tx and claim packages
guard the public planner lane so WorkItem construction cannot reopen outside ingress
residual_risk: No live safe-API bypass remains in z00z_aggregators; bypass would require stepping outside the public Rust API contract and is out of scope for this report.
